details: all 16 acceptance tests pass in --dev and non-dev; dual-mcast handshake clean under both timings
golden harness 20 passed / 1 skipped / 646 xfailed, no XPASS-strict drift; measured PCC=1.0 relRMS~0.0015

summary: R1 plan: widen SUPPORTED dtype/weight_dtype to {fp32,bf16,bf8b}, fp32_dest_acc_en to {True,False}. Program descriptor: (a) interm format = fp32 if acc else output dtype (matmul_block last-block pack reconfig gated on acc, so interm must match out when acc=False), (b) dtype-aware L1 footprint, (c) effective compute config clamping HiFi4->HiFi2 for bf16/bf8b inputs to dodge #38306 K-accumulator corruption on wormhole_b0. Keep {fp32,acc=False} EXCLUSION. Probe bf16+HiFi4 corruption empirically.
probe_003 NameError: device not defined — tt-probe.sh does not auto-inject device variable as assumed; need to open device manually in probe

probe_004 deep-K 256x2048x512: bf16 clamp acc=True PCC=0.999993; bf16 FORCED-HiFi4 acc=True PCC=0.999997 (no #38306 corruption on this shape); bf16 acc=False PCC=0.999611; bf8b acc=True PCC=0.999913; bf8b acc=False PCC=0.998461 (passes 0.98 — no EXCLUSION needed for this shape); bf16act/fp32wt PCC=0.999993. All within golden tolerance bands.
all dtype x acc combos pass golden tolerances on deep-K shape; clamp kept as conservative #38306 guard (zero precision cost)
20 passed — fp32 acceptance + baseline unchanged after dtype widening (fp32 path byte-identical)
15 passed (all dtype x acc combos), 3 xfail ({fp32,acc=False} EXCLUSION x3 weight_dtypes), no XPASS drift. Harness wiring correct.
full golden suite exceeded 2-min bash limit (not a device hang) — ~300 supported matmuls incl large/wide-K shapes. Re-run with longer timeout + log file.

286 passed, 14 failed (all severity=precision), 366 xfailed, 1 skipped, NO XPASS drift. All 14 failures = low-precision(bf16/bf8b)+acc=False+deep-K(>=4096). bf8b+acc=False: rms 0.18-0.39 vs 0.15. bf16+acc=False K=8192: rms 0.125-0.128 vs 0.10 (slim). bf16/bf8b at acc=True pass ALL shapes incl K=8192.

Deep-K acc=False failures are 16-bit DEST accumulation error ~O(sqrt(K)) (matmul_block header predicts). For bf8b, the dominant extra error is the bf8b interm spill (interm=out=bf8b forced by gate when acc=False). Cheap lever to try: bfp8_pack_precise=True (precise bf8b packing of partials). bf16 K=8192 slim miss has no lever besides acc=True. Probe bfp8_pack_precise on worst bf8b cases.

bfp8_pack_precise=True made ZERO difference on deep-K bf8b acc=False (rms identical 0.1833/0.3854). Confirms dominant error is 16-bit DEST accumulation, not bf8b packing. No descriptor-level lever fixes deep-K acc=False; only fp32_dest_acc_en=True does.
